entries.title: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna Goes Fully Public After 13-Day Government Review; Grok 4.5 Also Launches Without System Card
entries.summary: On July 9, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) became broadly available to all ChatGPT users and API developers after a 13-day government-coordinated preview, establishing what analysts call a de facto preclearance norm for frontier AI models; simultaneously, xAI launched Grok 4.5 publicly with only a single-sentence Musk claim of 'Opus-class' performance and no system card, benchmarks, or technical documentation.

links.title: GPT-5.6 Goes Public After 12-Day White House Gate (TechTimes)
AI News Today July 9 2026: GPT-5.6 and Grok 4.5 Launch Same Day (Build Fast With AI)

entries.title: Trump Abruptly Cancels Expected Voluntary AI EO Signing Ceremony; August 1 Deadline Remains Only Confirmed Governance Milestone
entries.summary: On July 8, President Trump cancelled a scheduled Oval Office signing ceremony for a new AI executive order governing pre-release model review, telling reporters he worried it could undermine America's lead over China, leaving the August 1 statutory deadline (under the June 2 EO) for NSA/CISA to deliver classified frontier model benchmarks as the only remaining firm governance milestone.

links.title: AI News Wed July 8 2026: Trump Cancels AI EO Signing (tech-reader.blog)
AI News Today July 8 2026 (Build Fast With AI)

entries.title: FTC Proposes 'Suppression of Accuracy' Policy: Undisclosed AI Output Steering May Violate Section 5 FTC Act
entries.summary: On July 1, the FTC published a proposed policy statement (open for comment through July 31) arguing that AI companies which secretly steer their systems' outputs toward undisclosed ideological objectives — rather than what users request — may be committing deception under Section 5 of the FTC Act; critically, the statement singles out Colorado's AI Act as potentially preempted by federal law where compliance would require suppressing accurate outputs.

links.title: FTC Proposed Policy Statement on AI Accuracy (FTC.gov)
Federal Register Notice: Policy Statement Concerning Suppression of Accuracy in AI Systems
FTC Seeks Comment on AI Accuracy Policy Statement (Inside Privacy)

entries.title: TechPolicy.Press H1 2026 State AI Report: 109 AI Laws Enacted Across States, AI Companion Chatbot Regulation Dominant Theme
entries.summary: A NYU Center on Technology Policy midyear analysis finds that U.S. states have enacted 109 AI laws and 28 data center laws as of July 1, 2026 — with AI companion chatbot safety the single most active area of regulation — and that bipartisan support for AI rules is spreading across state lines even as the Trump administration pushes for federal preemption.

links.title: Where State AI Legislation Stands Half Way Into 2026 (TechPolicy.Press)

entries.title: House Science Committee Marks Up 10 Bipartisan AI Bills in Single Session — Research, Cybersecurity, Workforce, Energy
entries.summary: The House Science, Space, and Technology Committee advanced ten bipartisan AI bills covering research access, AI cybersecurity, workforce development, transparency, and data center energy standards in a single markup session, signaling Congress is laying institutional groundwork for more substantive AI oversight ahead.

links.title: AI: The Washington Report — July 2026 Edition (Mintz)
AI: The Washington Report — July 2026 Edition (Mondaq)

entries.title: Warner Music v. Udio: Strategic Partnership Settlement Reached, Terms Confidential
entries.summary: Warner Music Group settled its copyright lawsuit against AI music generator Udio on confidential terms structured as a strategic partnership, according to AI lawsuit tracking sources, marking the second major music label to exit litigation in favor of a licensing arrangement with an AI music company.

entries.title: CNBC Confirms Chinese AI Models Now Account for 30–46% of US Enterprise API Token Usage
entries.summary: New CNBC data published July 8 confirms that Chinese AI models (led by Z.ai's GLM-5.2 and open-source DeepSeek variants) account for between 30% and 46% of enterprise API tokens flowing through US developer platforms like OpenRouter and Vercel — up from 4.5% in early 2025 — driven by price advantages of 60–90% versus OpenAI and Anthropic, raising national security and supply-chain concentration concerns.

links.title: AI News Today July 8 2026: Chinese AI Confirmed at 46% Enterprise Usage (Build Fast With AI)

entries.title: Meta Offers $250M to 24-Year-Old AI Researcher Matt Deitke — Largest Compensation Package for a Young Researcher in History
entries.summary: Meta reportedly offered PhD dropout and University of Washington researcher Matt Deitke a four-year, $250 million compensation package — the largest ever offered to a young AI researcher — after an initial $125 million offer was reportedly turned down, illustrating the extreme talent concentration dynamic at frontier labs.

links.title: AI News Briefs Bulletin Board for July 2026 (Radical Data Science)

entries.title: arXiv 2607.02201: Eticas AI Risk Taxonomy — Open Infrastructure for Operationalizing AI Audits
entries.summary: This paper addresses the fragmentation among 74+ AI risk taxonomies by proposing the Eticas framework as an operationalization layer that bridges risk naming to actual test execution, measured severity scoring, and defensible grades — demonstrated end-to-end on PII leakage against GPT-4, showing disclosure rates ranging from 0% to 84% under adversarial conditioning.

links.title: arXiv 2607.02201: The Eticas AI Risk Taxonomy

entries.title: arXiv 2607.07612: 'Towards Agentic AI Governance: A Preliminary Assessment' (AIR-RES 2026)
entries.summary: A new cs.CY/cs.AI preprint accepted to the AI Revolution Research, Ethics, and Society 2026 conference proposes a preliminary framework for governing agentic AI systems, addressing how governance mechanisms need to evolve as AI moves from static models to autonomous agents capable of multi-step action.

entries.title: Illinois AI Safety Measures Act Signed July 6: Key Clarifications on Scope, Penalties, and Industry Dynamics
entries.summary: New details from the Illinois AISMA signing confirm SB 315 applies to AI models generating over $500 million annually, mirrors California SB-53 and New York RAISE Act, imposes civil penalties of up to $3 million for repeat violations, and takes effect January 1, 2028 — with both OpenAI and Anthropic supporting the bill as a model for a coordinated state-led approach to frontier AI safety.

links.title: Illinois Sets National Standard for AI Regulation (MyStateline/WTVO)
Pritzker Signs Landmark AI Regulation Bill (WTTW)

entries.title: SCOTUS Elimination of Independent Federal Agencies Reshapes Options for AI Governance Institutional Design
entries.summary: Transformer News publishes an opinion arguing that the Supreme Court's ruling striking down independent federal agencies (the 'Slaughter ruling') paradoxically strengthens the case for independent third-party verification for AI governance — since a new independent AI regulator is now unconstitutional, external auditing and verification frameworks become the primary mechanism for accountability.

links.title: SCOTUS Killed the Independent Agency. AI Governance Doesn't Need One (Transformer News)
